revision.comment: Bot: remove [[Category:Sn crud]] (crud category cleanup) [[github:EmmaLeonhart/shintowiki-scripts/actions/runs/23984597483|manual triggered pipeline]]
revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:福井県護国神社]]
{{Referencesの明記|date=2017th year 8月28日 (月) 09:12 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = 福井県護国神社 | location = 福井県福井市大宮2丁目13-18 | coordinates = {{Coord|36|4|54.84|N|136|13|6.04|E|scale:20000}} | deity = 国事殉難者 | type = 内務大臣指定護国神社・別表神社 | established = 昭和16th year （1941st year ） | image = | festival = [[4月13日]]・[[11月2日]] }}
'''Fukui Prefecture Gokoku Shrine''' (福井県護国神社, Fukui-ken Gokoku Jinja) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|qid=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} ({{ill|Gokoku shrines|en|Gokoku shrines|qid=Q1534477|lt=Gokoku Shrine|1=Gokoku Shrines}}) located in {{ill|Fukui (city)|en|Fukui (city)|qid=Q201618|lt=Fukui, Fukui|1=Fukui (city)}}, {{ill|Fukui Prefecture|en|Fukui Prefecture|qid=Q133879|lt=Fukui Prefecture|1=Fukui Prefecture}}. The shrine crest (kamon) is a cherry blossom.
== Enshrined Deities == * The shrine enshrines about 32,000 spirits from Fukui Prefecture who died in national crises from the {{ill|Meiji Restoration|en|Meiji Restoration|qid=Q8707|lt=Meiji Restoration|1=Meiji Restoration}} to {{ill|World War II|en|World War II|qid=Q362|lt=World War II|1=World War II}}. This includes the Meiji Restoration patriot {{ill|Hashimoto Sanai|en|Hashimoto Sanai|qid=Q1036286|lt=Hashimoto Sanai|1=Hashimoto Sanai}}. * In the separate hall, the Kōan Reisha, police officers and others who died in the line of duty, casualties of the Manchurian Colonization Corps, and Self-Defense Force personnel are enshrined.
== History == * In 1939 (Shōwa 14), following the establishment of the Gokoku Shrine system, a support association was formed by the governor of Fukui Prefecture and others to establish the shrine. * The shrine buildings were completed in March 1941 (Shōwa 16), and the enshrinement ceremony was held, designating it as an official Gokoku Shrine by the Minister of Home Affairs. During the GHQ occupation after World War II, the shrine was renamed "Fukui Goryō-gū," but in 1955 (Shōwa 30), it reverted to its original name. * All buildings were destroyed by the {{ill|1948 Fukui earthquake|en|1948 Fukui earthquake|qid=Q4389555|lt=1948 Fukui earthquake|1=1948 Fukui earthquake}}. The main hall was rebuilt in 1951 (Shōwa 26), and other shrine buildings were reconstructed in 1959 (Shōwa 34). * In April 2007 (Heisei 19), a monument to the kamikaze pilots was erected.
== External Links == * [http://www.fukuigokoku.jp 福井県護国神社] (Official Website)
{{shinto-stub}}
